methods.description: This method involves processing the core data to remove reworked material and outliers, followed by performing a principal component analysis (PCA) to detect different lithological facies using inorganic geochemical data. The PCA helps in reducing the number of variables and objectively describing differences and similarities between elements.

steps.description: Core sequences with reworked material were removed from the original data set.
steps.searchable_summary: remove reworked material from core data

steps.description: High amounts of resin were detected by a locally low sum of measured intensities and are subsequently removed.
steps.searchable_summary: remove high resin content from data

steps.description: A principal component analysis (PCA) was performed to detect different lithological facies using inorganic geochemical data.
Data are resampled after smoothing using a Gaussian kernel filter with a 20 point window size.
steps.searchable_summary: resample and smooth data with Gaussian filter

steps.description: Data were log transformed and z-normalized to zero mean and unit standard deviation for each parameter.
Outliers were removed by deleting values greater/less than mean plus/minus three times standard deviation.

methods.description: This method involves scanning core sections for selected elements using an energy dispersive X-ray fluorescence scanner. The method measures major and trace elements and evaluates the suitability of quantification using the standard free fundamental parameter method.

steps.description: The three core sections were scanned for selected elements at 1 mm resolution using the energy dispersive X-ray fluorescence scanner Eagle III.
steps.searchable_summary: scan core sections for elements using XRF

steps.description: The µXRF was used to measure major and trace elements on 10 cm long resin impregnated samples.
steps.searchable_summary: measure major and trace elements using µXRF

steps.description: Discrete reference samples are taken from core DE3 and measured by a Philips MagiXPRO via WD-XRF method.
Examine whether a quantification of the Eagle III µXRF using the standard free fundamental parameter method is suitable.
